aromaphile.name: Aromaphile
description: ## Aromaphile
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/18›
Rather than employ the baleful glares of the typical mesmerist, aromaphiles hypnotize others using an array of sickeningly sweet scents developed from their unique ghoran physiology.
::prereq{r=Ghoran}

::ab[Hypnotic Aroma (Su)]{icon=aura-lower swift="An aromaphile can use unique aspects of ghoran biology to release intoxicating aromas in a 10-foot-radius ‹misc/burst› centered on him. All creatures within this area, as well as creatures with ‹umr/scent› within 30 feet, take a -2 penalty on Will saving throws. The aroma hovers around the aromaphile, moving with him, and lasts for 1 minute." imp8="This penalty becomes -3." special="The hypnotic aroma must be inhaled to be effective, and creatures that choose to ‹misc/hold their breath›, do not breathe, or are immune to inhaled poisons are immune to its effect." replace="Hypnotic stare~painful stare~towering ego"}

::ab[Debilitating Aroma (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="An aromaphile can imbue his hypnotic aroma with another effect, chosen from those listed within the *bold stare* mesmerist class feature. However, they affect creatures affected by his *hypnotic aroma* for as long as they are affected by that ability." repeat="The aromaphile selects a new *debilitating aroma.*~3~4" special="The aromaphile chooses one option each time he gains a new debilitating aroma improvement, and the choice can't be changed later. The aromaphile can't choose the same debilitating aroma more than once unless otherwise noted." replace="Bold stare"}

:::ab{l=3 icon=touch-aid-lower title="Aromatic Manipulation (Su)" action=Ability alter="Touch treatment" replace="Mental potency" special="The target can attempt a Will save to negate these ill effects (DC = 10 + half the aromaphile's level + the aromaphile's Charisma modifier). On a failed save, the target gains the chosen condition for a number of rounds equal to the aromaphile's level. Each round that the target is affected, it can attempt a new Will save at the same DC to end the effect early. If the target succeeds at such a save, its turn immediately ends." imp6="His touch can deaden the target's emotions (as per ‹spell/calm emotions›)." imp10="His touch can cause the target to become ‹misc/stunned›." imp14="His touch can cause the target to fall asleep (as per ‹spell/deep slumber›, except the Hit Die limit is equal to the aromaphile's level + 2)."}

The aromaphile can touch a creature affected by his *hypnotic aroma* to alter its mental state. This functions as the *touch treatment* class feature, except the aromaphile cannot remove the *confused, cowering,* or *stunned* conditions, and he cannot *break enchantments* using this ability. Instead, he adds ‹misc/fatigued› to his list of minor conditions and ‹misc/exhausted› to his list of greater conditions.

An aromaphile can also use this ability to inflict hampering conditions on a target by touching it. Initially, he can cause his target to become ‹misc/fascinated› by his *aroma,* but he gains more abilities as he gains levels.

autohypnotist.name: Autohypnotist
description: ## Autohypnotist
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/17›
An autohypnotist has incredibly powerful psychic abilities that he can't entirely control. As a result, maintaining his powers against an opponent hinders him as much as his target.
:::ab{l=1 icon=boost-down title="Autohypnosis (Su)" action=Ability alter="Hypnotic stare"}

An autohypnotist has mastered techniques that make his *hypnotic stare* ability more potent, but at a terrible cost - the autohypnotist succumbs to his own power when he uses it. Whenever the autohypnotist focuses his stare on an opponent, the penalties that opponent takes from the *hypnotic stare* (as well as any *bold stare* improvements or stare feats that the autohypnotist has) increase by 1. However, the autohypnotist takes the same penalties as his target while focusing his stare, including those of any *bold stare* improvements he has.

An autohypnotist can reduce or negate the penalty he imposes upon himself with his *hypnotic stare,* but doing so allows his opponent an opportunity to escape from the stare as well. The autohypnotist can reduce the penalty he takes from his *hypnotic stare* ability by ‹misc/half› for 1 round as a @HL[swift action]. If he does so, there is a 25% chance that his *hypnotic stare* ends altogether. Alternatively, he can choose to negate the penalty he takes from his *hypnotic stare* for 1 round, but his *hypnotic stare* then has a 50% chance of ending.

::ab[Wide Stare (Su)]{l=5 icon=power-aura standard="Whenever an autohypnotist is focusing his stare on a foe, he can broaden his *hypnotic stare.* When he does so, all creatures within 10 feet of his opponent take penalties from the mesmerist's *hypnotic stare* and *bold stare* class features as if he were maintaining his *hypnotic stare* against each of those creatures, except such creatures treat his *hypnotic stare* penalty as if it were 2 lower." special="When the autohypnotist uses this ability, he cannot exclude allies or any other creatures from this effect. If the autohypnotist's *hypnotic stare* ends for the focused opponent, this effect ends." replace="Mental potency"}

chart_caster.name: Chart Caster
description: ## Chart Caster
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/10›

Lirgeni astrologists would divine their futures by consulting star charts, but some had no talent for astrology and simply faked their results. When prophecy died with Aroden, a few Lirgeni survivors continued with this "reliable" method.
::cskill{gain=kna~kp}

:::ab{icon=down-power action=Ability title="Subject of the Stars (Su)" alter="Mesmerist tricks~manifold tricks" special="Using this ability again on an existing subject or to implant any trick on a new subject ends all previously implanted tricks. The caster can trigger only one instance of the same trick on a given subject per round." incrementPlain="A chart caster can~1~4" incrementDesc=implant incrementEnd=" different subjects with multiple instances of one trick each and can choose a different trick for each subject."}

A chart caster cannot implant tricks as a standard action. Instead, he can spend 5 minutes to implant multiple tricks in a single willing target. The number of tricks he can implant per day is unchanged from the regular mesmerist class, but he can apply as many of those tricks as he chooses on a single use of this ability.

If implanting tricks in himself, the caster merely spends 5 minutes in contemplation; otherwise, he consults star charts while ritually asking the creature questions, and still must touch them to do so. He can select multiple instances of the same trick to implant in a single subject.

::ab[Feign Destiny (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost-protect immediate="A chart caster can grant an ally a +1 competence bonus on a failed attack roll, saving throw, or skill check by reminding his ally of their destined success. The chart caster must use his ability within 1 round of the failed check, and his ally must be within 30 feet of him when he does. If the bonus is enough to turn the failure into a success, the ally succeeds at the check." incrementAt="The bonus~6~10~2/1" useMod=Charisma replace="Touch treatment"}

cult_master.name: Cult Master
description: ## Cult Master
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/96›

The leaders of some cults are simply charlatans, bathing in the adulation of their followers. Others believe themselves to be truly in touch with an unknown power, bringing their unique messages to those willing to follow and obey them.
::ab[Insidious Personality (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A cult master adds 1/2 his class level (minimum 1) as a bonus on all Diplomacy checks." replace="Consummate liar"}

::ab[Fanatical Stare (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost-protect order=ap flavor="A cult master can turn his gaze on a creature to fill it with fervent belief in the cult master." ability="The target of the gaze gains a competence bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-sixth› of his mesmerist level." passive="Instead of taking the *hypnotic stare's* penalty on Will saves, the target gains a bonus of an equal amount on Will saves. The bonuses don't apply to attacks against the cult master or on saves against the cult master's spells or abilities. The cult master can also suppress any penalties inflicted by his stare for the target." replace="Painful stare"}
::ab[Cult Tricks (Su)]{head icon=power-info note="The following new tricks can be selected by a cult master. These tricks can be implanted only in a cohort or follower of the cult master."}

::ab[Double]{next icon=def ability="An illusion makes either the cult master's cohort or one of his followers appear to be him. The cult master can trigger this trick when he is targeted by an attack, provided he is adjacent to the subject, to give the attack a 50% miss chance. If the attack misses the cult master, it targets the subject instead. This is an illusion (glamer) effect."}

::ab[Extol]{next icon=boost ability="A mental compulsion causes a cohort or follower to extol the virtues of the cult master. The cult master can trigger this trick whenever he attempts a Diplomacy check, as long as he is within 60 feet of the subject. The subject takes the ‹rule/aid another› action on the cult master's Diplomacy check. If the subject is successful, the cult master gains a +4 bonus on his check. This is an enchantment (compulsion) effect." alter="Mesmerist tricks"}

::ab[False Healing (Su)]{l=3 icon=aid useMod=Charisma3 flavor="A cult master can provide temporary healing by using his psychic powers to trick the mind." standard="The cult master must be able to touch his target. The cult master grants a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to 1d8 + his Charisma modifier to the target." imp6="The cult master can either grant a number of temporary hit points equal to 2d8 + his Charisma modifier to the target or temporarily remove 1d4 points of ‹rule/ability damage›." special="This can be used as a @HLswift_action if the cult master uses it on himself. A creature can receive the effect of the *false healing* only once per day. The temporary hit points or reduction in ability damage lasts a number of hours equal to the cult master's class level." replace="3rd- and 6th-level touch treatments"}

::ab[Faithful Followers (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power flavor="A cult master begins to attract followers to his espoused beliefs, thanks to his magnetic personality." ability="The cult master gains ‹feat/Leadership› as a bonus feat." replace="7th-level bold stare"}

::ab[Fanatical Devotion (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost flavor="A cult master has manipulated his cohort and followers into becoming utterly devoted to him." passive="The cult master no longer takes a penalty to his Leadership score for being aloof or cruel. Additionally, the DCs of all enchantment spells the cult master casts on his cohort and followers increase by 4." imp14="The cult master no longer takes a penalty to his Leadership score for any cohorts or followers he has killed or caused to be killed." replace="10th- and 14th-level touch treatments"}

::ab[Masterful Cult Tricks (Su)]{head prereq="12th-level mesmerist" icon=power note="The following new master tricks can be selected by any cult master of 12th level or higher. These tricks can be implanted only in a cohort or follower of the cult master." alter="Masterful tricks"}

::ab[Avenge Me]{next icon=boost ability="The cult master creates a compulsion for one of his followers to avenge him. Whenever the cult master takes hit point damage from an attack, he can trigger this trick as long as he is within 60 feet of the subject. The subject is immediately affected as if by the ‹spell/rage› spell for 1 round per mesmerist level."}

::ab[Die for Me]{next icon=def ability="The cult master instills within either his cohort or one of his followers the desire to give her life for him. The cult master can trigger this trick whenever he would take hit point damage from an attack, spell, or effect and the subject is with 60 feet of him. Half the damage is psychically transferred to the subject. This is an enchantment (compulsion) effect."}

::ab[Reborn (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-magic flavor="A cult master can ensure his own survival upon his death." ability="The cult master can perform a 1-hour ceremony costing 5,000 gp in rare materials in which he uses his psychic powers to implant a small portion of his essence into his cohort, who must be a minimum of 17th level. When the cult master dies, in 7 days the cohort physically and mentally transforms into the cult master as if ‹spell/clone› had been cast, except that the cohort's body takes the place of the inert body." special="The cult master can't use this ability again until he gains a new cohort of at least 17th level and performs the necessary ceremony. The cohort can't be brought back to life except through ‹spell/miracle› or ‹spell/wish›." replace="Rule minds"}

dreamstalker.name: Dreamstalker
description: ## Dreamstalker
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/60›
Many mesmerists concentrate their studies on conscious, rational thought, but dreamstalkers instead delve into the unleashed unconscious mind that wanders free in dreams.
::ab[Dreams of Pain]{icon=down note="A dreamstalker's *painful stare* deals nonlethal damage." alter="Painful stare"}

::ab[Somnomancer]{l=1 icon=power-learn-magic ability="A dreamstalker gains the ‹hex/slumber› hex. Furthermore, when she would normally gain a new mesmerist trick, she can learn additional spells instead. Each time she does so, she adds two of the following spells of a level she can currently cast to her list of spells known." s1="Oneiric horror~~sleep" s2="Detect mindscape~~greater oneiric horror~~instigate psychic duel" s3="Deep slumber~~minor dream" s4="Create mindscape~~dream~~forgetful slumber~~mindscape door~~sleepwalk~~sleepwalking suggestion" s5="Cloak of dreams~~dream council~~dream scan~~symbol of sleep~~waves of fatigue" s6="Dream travel~~greater create mindscape~~waves of exhaustion" special="The dreamstalker's effective witch level is equal to her mesmerist level, and she can activate a hex only by expending two daily uses of mesmerist tricks. The save DCs for her hexes are Charisma-based." imp10="She can take the ‹hex/nightmares› or ‹hex/vision› hex instead of gaining a new mesmerist trick." imp18="She can select the ‹hex/eternal slumber› hex in place of a mesmerist trick." alter="Mesmerist tricks" replace="1st-level mesmerist trick"}

::ab[Sleepless (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A dreamstalker gains immunity to sleep effects and a bonus equal to her Charisma bonus on saving throws against ‹misc/fatigue› and ‹misc/exhaustion›. She loses both benefits when under an effect that would prevent her from providing an ‹rule/emotion component›." replace="Towering ego"}

::ab[Touch of Night (Su)]{icon=down l=3 ability="A dreamstalker can't remove shaken, frightened, sickened, cowering, nauseated, or panicked conditions using her *touch treatment.* However, she can remove fatigue or magical sleep (other than hard-to-remove magical sleep such as the *eternal slumber* hex) as a minor condition, ‹misc/staggered› as a moderate condition, and exhausted as a greater condition. As normal, removing fatigue or exhaustion only removes the condition, not the underlying cause, such as lack of sleep." alter="Touch treatment"}

::ab[Dreamwalker (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A dreamstalker treats ‹rule/dreamscape«s› as a ‹ability/favored terrain›, as per the ‹class/ranger› ability, gaining a +2 bonus that increases by 2 every 4 mesmerist levels after 5th. While in a dreamscape, she also treats any creature from the ‹rule/Dimension of Dreams<_oa› (including dreamed figments) as her ‹ability/favored enemy›, with a bonus equal to 1/2 her favored terrain bonus." replace="Manifold tricks"}
::ab[Tramautic Spell]{l=11 icon=power ability="A dreamstalker gains ‹feat/Traumatic Spell› as a bonus feat." replace="Glib lie"}

enigma.name: Enigma
description: ## Enigma
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/36›

An enigma spends his life dedicated to developing psychic abilities that allow him to operate unnoticed. While the target of a mesmerist's *hypnotic stare* might not realize that the mesmerist is the source of the strange thoughts invading his mind, an enigma takes that mental obfuscation to a more extreme level. Enigmas often end up serving as master spies and infiltration experts.

::ab[Solipsism (Su)]{l=1 icon=def ability="Whenever the enigma uses *hypnotic stare* on a creature, instead of applying a penalty on the creature's saving throws, the enigma begins to fade from the creature's view. Until the enigma's next turn, the enigma gains the effects of ‹rule/concealment› against that creature (unless it can see invisible creatures). Starting on the enigma's next turn, he gains the effect of ‹spell/invisibility› against that creature. These effects last as long as the enigma continues to use his *hypnotic stare,* but if he takes an action that would end *invisibility,* it ends his *hypnotic stare* immediately. The enigma can reinstate this effect whenever he wishes, but each time it begins with 1 round of concealment." imp8="Attacks that would end *invisibility* do not end the enigma's *hypnotic stare,* and after 1 round of concealment, he gains the benefits of ‹spell/greater invisibility› against the target of his stare." special="For the purpose of *bold stare* improvements, the enigma's *hypnotic stare* always has a penalty of -1." alter="Hypnotic stare"}
::ab[Veiled Steps]{icon=boost passive="An enigma gains a bonus to his Stealth checks equal to 1/4 his mesmerist level (minimum +1)." replace="Consummate liar"}

::ab[Enigmatic Stare (Su)]{icon=lower passive="The target of an enigma's *hypnotic stare* takes a -4 penalty on Perception checks to notice the enigma." imp8="This penalty increases to - 6." replace="Painful stare"}

::ab[Transfer Affliction (Su)]{l=3 icon=touch standard="The enigma can afflict others with the harmful conditions that he endures. This ability functions as the *touch treatment* ability, but can only be used to remove harmful conditions from the enigma, not other creatures. In order to remove these harmful effects, the enigma must succeed at a touch attack against another creature. The creature touched gains the chosen harmful effect for whatever duration it would have afflicted the enigma and the effect is removed from the enigma, unless the creature succeeds at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the enigma's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier). Using this ability is always a standard action." imp14="The enigma can instead expend one use of *touch treatment* to replicate the effects of ‹spell/bestow curse›, delivered as a touch attack as part of using this ability." special="*Transfer affliction* is a mind-affecting effect." alter="Touch treatment"}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="An enigma gains the ‹ability/sneak attack› ability of a rogue, starting at +1d6 damage." increment="Sneak attack damage~5~4" incrementEnd="d6." replace="Manifold tricks"}

::ab[Detection Void (Su)]{l=11 icon=def ability="An enigma can elude ‹main/detect«ion» spells› (spells with &quot&detect&quot& in their name). A creature using this sort of magic against the enigma must succeed at a caster level check against a DC equal to 15 + the enigma's class level to succeed (as if the enigma was under the effect of a ‹spell/nondetection› spell); failure means the magic fails to detect the enigma, as if he did not exist." replace="Glib lie"}

::ab[Absentia (Su)]{l=20 icon=lower passive="All creatures adjacent to the enigma subconsciously ignore him. Adjacent creatures are always treated as ‹rule/flat-footed› to the enigma and are not considered to be directly observing the enigma for the purpose of the Stealth skill. This effect ends immediately if the enigma makes a successful attack against any creature, but automatically resumes at the beginning of his next turn." replace="Rule minds"}

eyebiter.name: Eyebiter
description: ## Eyebiter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/36›

Mesmerists focus their abilities through the eyes, drawing forth their power as the windows to the mind and soul. Eyebiters take this a step further. An eyebiter's eyes become so infused with psychic might that they can leave the eyebiter's body and move about on their own.

::ab[Eyeball Familiar (Ex)]{icon=power-down ability="An eyebiter imbues one of his eyes with limited sentience, so that he can pluck it out and allow it to move on its own. The *eyeball familiar* functions similarly to the ‹discovery/tumor familiar› alchemist discovery except as follows. The *eyeball familiar* does not have fast healing. The *eyeball familiar* doesn't resemble an animal, and its size is Fine for a Medium or smaller eyebiter (or one size category larger for every size category the eyebiter is larger than Medium). It has a fly speed of 20 feet (perfect maneuverability) and no physical attacks (though it can still deliver touch spells once the eyebiter has reached 3rd level). The eyeball has a Strength score of 1, a Dexterity score of 12, and a Constitution score of 10. It has no set Hit Die, base saves, or skill ranks of its own, though it uses its master's when a familiar normally would. It doesn't grant its master any familiar bonus, nor can it ever be an *improved familiar.*" special="Whenever the eyebiter's *eyeball familiar* leaves his body, he is ‹misc/dazzled› until it returns (if it is destroyed, the eyebiter loses that eye until he gains the effects of a ‹spell/regenerat«ion<e›). An eyebiter must have at least one eye to use this ability, and an eyebiter with only one eye is ‹misc/blinded› instead of dazzled until his familiar returns." replace="Consummate liar~mesmerist trick gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Ocular Occlusion (Su)]{head l=3 icon=touch-zap useMod=Charisma3 standard="An eyebiter gains the ability to cloud the sight of others with a melee touch attack. A successful Will save negates the effect (DC = 10 + 1/2 the eyebiter's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier); this is a mind-affecting ability." special="If the eyebiter chooses to use an *ocular occlusion* of a lower level than the highest-level effect he has gained, he can instead use a standard action to target a creature within 30 feet with that effect as a ranged touch attack." replace="All instances of touch treatment"}

::ab[Clouded Vision]{next l=3 icon=lower ability="The eyebiter can cause the target to become dazzled for 1 minute and suffer a 20% miss chance on all attacks the target makes against opponents within 60 feet. Opponents more than 60 feet away have ‹rule/total »concealment› from the target."}
::ab[Cause or Cure Blindness]{next l=6 icon=lower-aid ability="The eyebiter can blind the target for 1 minute or affect it as per the spell ‹spell/remove blindness›."}

::ab[Eyeless Horror]{next l=10 icon=lower ability="The eyebiter creates a powerful illusion that causes the target's eyes to appear as if they have been ripped out, with blood pouring from the sockets. The target is blinded and ‹misc/shaken› for 1 round per the eyebiter's mesmerist level, taking 1d4 points of nonlethal damage each round. The target can attempt a second Will save when attacked to negate the shaken effect and the nonlethal damage."}

::ab[Spellblight]{next l=14 icon=lower ability="The eyebiter can afflict the target with the *ebon eyes* or the *hemoculysis* ‹rule/minor spellblight<s›, or the *eldritch cataracts* ‹rule/major spellblight<s›."}

::ab[Staring Eye (Su)]{l=5 icon=power-magic ability="An eyebiter can expend one use of his daily mesmerist tricks to either see through his *eyeball familiar* as if it were an ‹spell/arcane eye›, or to use his *hypnotic stare* (including related abilities like *painful stare* and *bold stare*), as well as any ‹umr/gaze› attack he possesses (like ‹spell/burning gaze› and ‹spell/eyebite›) through his *eyeball familiar,* as if he were at its location." replace="Mental potency"}

::ab[Omnivisual (Ex)]{l=11 icon=def-power passive="An eyebiter's roving *eyeball familiar* is constantly watching in all directions. As long as his *eyeball familiar* is outside his body and near enough to see the same things (such as being in the same room), the eyebiter gains ‹umr/all-around vision› and cannot be ‹rule/flanked›." replace="Glib lie"}

fey_trickster.name: Fey Trickster
description: ## Fey Trickster
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/37›

Most mesmerists draw psychic power from the Astral Plane, but a few gain their powers in an unusual way: from a brush with the fey. Prisoner-guests of the fey, these souls find themselves changed when they return to the mortal world, infused with the eldritch energy of their former hosts.
::cskill{gain=ha~kna~sur lose=app~kd~prof}

::ab[Nature's Lure]{icon=magic ability="Rather than casting psychic spells from the mesmerist spell list, a fey trickster casts divine spells drawn from the ‹main/druid and ranger »spell« list»s<_hunter›, in just the same way as a hunter does. This means that she gains orisons instead of knacks. This doesn't change her number of spells per day or spells known. The fey trickster's mesmerist spells and spell-like abilities count as if they were fey spell-like abilities for the purpose of effects like *resist nature's lure* that apply against fey spell-like abilities." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Feytouched (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def-power ability="A fey trickster's time with the fey has granted her strange powers, as well as resistance to further fey mischief. The fey trickster gains the druid's ‹ability/resist nature's lure› and ‹ability/woodland stride› abilities, and she counts as both ‹type/fey› and her original creature type for the purpose of spells and effects." replace="Towering ego"}

::ab[Fey Veil (Sp, Su)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="A fey trickster's physical form begins to shift, as she gains some of the impossible allure of the fey. At the same time, she begins able to use *fey veils* to assist herself and her allies in befuddling and bewitching their opponents." useMod=Charisma3 ability="Using the ability is a @HLstandard_action (or a @HLswift_action if the fey trickster calls up a *veil* over herself). If she targets another ally, the fey trickster must be able to contact the ally bodily, usually with a kiss. When she uses her *fey veil,* the fey trickster subtly alters the appearance and aura of her target, granting one of the powers allowed by her mesmerist level." imp3="She can grant the ally a +2 circumstance bonus on her choice of Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Handle Animal checks for 1 minute." imp6="She can change the ally's form so thoroughly that the ally gains a +10 bonus on Disguise checks until the next sunrise (similar to the bonus from ‹spell/disguise self›, but it also disguises touch and sound, and it doesn't allow a saving throw to disbelieve)." imp10="The fey trickster can make the ally and her aura so attractive or repulsive for 10 minutes that the *veil* automatically adjusts the moods of those who see the ally and fail their saving throw, as ‹spell/telempathic projection›." imp14="A fey trickster can create a *veil* so powerful that it fools even reality, granting the full effects of both the 6th-level *fey veil* and ‹spell/alter self› to the target for 1 hour. Each of these abilities, even the 14th-level ability, is an illusion and glamer effect, though the last ability counts as a size-changing ‹spelldef/polymorph› effect for the limit of only one polymorph or size-changing effect on a creature." replace="Touch treatment"}

::ab[One with the Fey]{head l=20 icon=magic-power passive="A fey trickster fully transforms into a fey, though she can still choose to count as her original creature type as well whenever it would be advantageous to her (such as if she is a humanoid and wishes to be affected by ‹spell/reduce person›). She gains low-light vision as well as an additional ability depending on which type of fey she chooses to most closely associate with. Some examples follow." replace="Rule minds"}
::ab[Erlking]{next icon=magic passive="The fey trickster is constantly under the effects of ‹spell/haste›."}
::ab[Nymph]{next icon=power ability="The fey trickster gains the ‹monster/nymph«'s› *unearthly grace* ability"}
::ab[Quickling]{next icon=power ability="The fey trickster gains the ‹monster/quickling«'s› *supernatural speed* ability."}
::ab[Rusalka]{next icon=melee ability="The fey trickster gains the ‹monster/rusalka«'s› *tresses* ability and four tress primary ‹umr/natural attacks› similar to those of a rusalka."}

gaslighter.name: Gaslighter
description: ## Gaslighter
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/60›
A gaslighter takes joy in others' suffering, delighting in driving them to madness.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning note="A gaslighter must be of an evil alignment. A gaslighter who becomes nonevil cannot gain new levels as a mesmerist but retains all his class abilities."}

::ab[Consummate Cruelty]{icon=boost passive="A gaslighter gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his mesmerist level (minimum 1) on Intimidate checks." ability="A gaslighter can take ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› and ‹feat/Greater Dirty Trick› without having ‹feat/Combat Expertise› or an Intelligence score of 13. He can also ignore those two prerequisites for other feats that have Improved Dirty Trick or Greater Dirty Trick as a prerequisite." order=pa replace="Consummate liar"}

::ab[Horrid Mask (Su)]{l=3 icon=lower-magic-boost useMod=Charisma3 standard="A gaslighter can implant a maddening phantasm in the mind of the target of his *hypnotic stare,* causing the target to perceive its own reflection as corrupted in some disturbing way. This is a mind-affecting fear effect, and there is no saving throw. The target is simultaneously captivated and horrified by the sight of its own reflection, and in any round in which the target can see itself in a mirror within 30 feet, the gaslighter can trigger his *painful stare* against the target without dealing damage. This is a ‹umr/gaze› attack, and the creature can avert its eyes to attempt to avoid it." special="Any creature holding a visible mirror that attempts to demoralize the target can choose to add the gaslighter's Charisma modifier to its Intimidate check instead of its own. *Horrid mask* lasts until that particular *hypnotic stare* ends (often when the target moves away from a mirror). The gaslighter can use this ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier." replace="Touch treatment (minor)" alter="Painful stare"}

::ab[Phantasmal Force (Su)]{l=6 icon=lower ability="A gaslighter can force the target of his *horrid mask* ability to recoil from its reflection. Whenever the gaslighter uses *horrid mask,* if an affected target begins its turn adjacent to a mirror, it must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the gaslighter's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier) or be compelled to either flee from the mirror (or the creature holding it) to beyond 30 feet or attempt to destroy the mirror (or the creature holding it). This is a gaze attack, and the target can attempt to avert its eyes." replace="Touch treatment (moderate)"}

::ab[Phantasmal Foes (Su)]{l=10 icon=lower standard="A gaslighter can spend one use of his *horrid mask* ability to convince the target of his *hypnotic stare* that it is surrounded by foes only it can see. The target is treated as ‹rule/flanked› for any melee attacks against it until the *hypnotic stare* ends." special="The gaslighter can use either this ability or *horrid mask* during a particular *hypnotic stare,* but not both at once. This is a mind-affecting fear effect." replace="Touch treatment (greater)"}

::ab[Corosion of Sanity (Su)]{l=14 icon=lower passive="Whenever a creature fails its initial saving throw against a gaslighter's single-target mind-affecting effect, the target also takes 1 point of ‹rule/Wisdom damage›. This has no effect on abilities that don't allow a saving throw or affect multiple targets or an area." special="If using ‹rule/the »sanity« system›, the target takes 2 points of sanity damage instead." replace="Touch treatment (break enchantment)"}

hate_monger.name: Hate-Monger
description: ## Hate-Monger
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/61›
Hate-mongers are xenophobic and judgmental rabblerousers who whip up mobs against enemies real or imagined.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning ability="A hate-monger must be of a nongood alignment. A hate-monger who becomes good cannot gain new levels as a mesmerist but retains all his class abilities."}

::ab[Mental Manipulator (Ex)]{icon=learn-boost ability="A hate-monger masters a broad repertoire of emotional corruption. At 1st level, he adds one spell from the 1st-level spells below to his spells known. When he would gain a new mesmerist trick, he can learn additional spells instead, adding two of the following spells of a level he can currently cast to his spells known. When he casts a spell he learns in this way, he increases the duration as if his caster level were 1 higher." s1="Cause fear~~compel hostility~~delusional pride~~demand offering~~forbid action~~lesser confusion~~murderous command~~scare~~sow thought" s2="Aversion~~paranoia~~rage~~unadulterated loathing" s3="Confusion~~crushing despair~~curse of disgust~~fear~~instant enemy~~malicious spite~~smug narcissism~~spite" s4="Envious urge~~feast on fear~~greater forbid action~~modify memory~~utter contempt" s5="Foe to friend~~foster hatred~~song of discord~~repress memory~~unwilling shield~~vengeful outrage" s6="Eyebite~~insanity~~overwhelming presence~~symbol of persuasion" imp5="The save DC is increased by 1 for creatures of his favored enemy type (see *xenophobe* below)." replace="Consummate liar" alter="Mesmerist tricks"}

::ab[Insidious Hatred (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic flavor="A hate-monger can sow hatred and strife under the pretense of assisting others." ability="He can use his *touch treatment* only on others, but as part of the action of using *touch treatment,* he can cast any targeted spell from the *mental manipulator* list that does not have a range of &quot&personal&quot& and has a casting time of 1 standard action or less, targeting only the creature that receives his *touch treatment.* The target creature takes a -2 penalty on its saving throw and to its spell resistance against the spell." special="That creature doesn't realize the hatemonger has cast a spell, and considers any manifestations to be part of the *touch treatment.* Other creatures notice as normal." alter="Touch treatment"}

::ab[Xenophobe (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A hate-monger can choose one ‹ability/favored enemy›, as per the ‹class/ranger› class feature. His favored enemy bonus begins at +2, but he does not gain additional favored enemies. His favored enemy bonus also applies on Intimidate checks against his favored enemy." increment="The bonus~5~4~4~2" replace="Mental potency~manifold tricks"}

::ab[Out for Blood (Ex)]{l=7 icon=lower passive="When a creature takes extra damage from the hate-monger's *painful stare,* it also takes 1 point of ‹rule/bleed› damage (or 1 point of bleed damage per 1d6 points of damage dealt by the *painful stare* if the hatemonger damages the target himself). This bleed damage does not stack with itself or other sources of bleed damage." replace="7th-level bold stare"}

::ab[Insidious Emotions (Su)]{l=11 icon=def passive="The power of a hatemonger's psychic hold becomes more difficult to detect or to break. The DC of Sense Motive checks to notice if a creature is under the effects of his enchantments with the emotion descriptor increases by 5 (if he also has the ‹feat/Subtle Enchantments› feat, the effects stack), as does the DC to identify his emotion spells. Magical divinations can detect his emotion effects only if the caster succeeds at a caster level check (DC = 11 the hate-monger's mesmerist level) as if the affected creatures were the subject of ‹spell/nondetection›." replace="Glib lie"}

material_manipulator.name: Material Manipulator
description: ## Material Manipulator
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/18›
Rather than waste time limiting himself to ruling minds, a material manipulator forces his whims upon reality and uses his magic to ensure that those around him comply with his demands.

::ab[Manipulator Spells]{icon=learn ability="A material manipulator casts psychic spells drawn from the mesmerist spell list and augmented by a select list of ‹main/spells>sorcerer»/wizard« spells›. All ‹main/spells_>illusion› (glamer), illusion (shadow), and ‹main/spells_>transmutation› spells of 6th level and lower are considered to be part of the material manipulator's spell list. These are psychic spells." alter=Spellcasting replace="Consummate liar~mesmerist trick gained at 2nd level~manifold tricks"}

::ab[Revision (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic useMod=Charisma3 flavor="A material manipulator can revise a creature's body to change its appearance." ability="Using *revision* is a @HLstandard_action (or a @HLswift_action if the material manipulator uses it on himself), and the material manipulator must be able to touch his target. An unwilling target must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + half the material manipulator's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier) to negate the effect. This effect lasts for 10 minutes per mesmerist level." imp3="The material manipulator can adjust the target's height and weight, increasing it or decreasing it to any height or weight within the standard range for creatures of the target's ‹rule/type and size category»<height_and_weight›." imp6="He can adjust the target's gender, physical traits (such as nose size, fingernail length, and foot shape), and pigmentation (including eye, hair, and skin color) to any natural variation for creatures of the target's type (and heritage, if applicable)." imp10="He can adjust the target's age, rewinding the target's age to as young as a young adult (approximately 8 years old for a human) or progressing it to as old as venerable. This causes the target to gain aging bonuses and penalties and adjust its size ‹rule/as appropriate for its new »age›, but doesn't confer any other benefits gained from aging (such as additional racial Hit Dice, supernatural abilities, and so on). The effects of *revision* now last for 1 hour per mesmerist level." imp14="He can adjust the target's type or subtype, as per ‹spell/polymorph›. This *revision* only lasts 1 minute per mesmerist level." imp20="He can instead adjust the target's type as per ‹spell/greater polymorph›. The effect of *revision* lasts indefinitely, or 10 minutes per mesmerist level if the material manipulator uses it as *greater polymorph* and causes the target to assume a form with a different type than its true form." special="The mesmerist can dismiss this effect as a @HLfree_action. This ability is a polymorph effect, and the subject of a *revision* cannot end the effect early by any means unless the material manipulator permits it. The material manipulator can stack multiple adjustments from this ability into the same polymorph effect - for instance, a 14th-level material manipulator can adjust himself into an orc, reduce his age to that of a young adult, give himself large feet and a bulbous nose, and change his gender. Clothing and equipment worn by the target is not affected by this effect - gear does not resize to suit the wearer's new form, nor does it merge into her body. Gear unable to be worn or wielded as a result of this effect falls to the ground in the wearer's space." replace="Touch treatment~rule minds"}

mindwyrm_mesmer.name: Mindwyrm Mesmer
description: ## Mindwyrm Mesmer
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/13›

While most mesmerists are subtle and manipulative schemers, mindwyrm mesmers emulate the confidence, swagger, and fear inspired by dragons to bully and browbeat others into obedience. These dragon-inspired psychic magic-users typically employ draconic imagery in their dress and the manifestations of their powers.

::ab[Innate Coercion]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A mindwyrm mesmer gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his mesmerist level (minimum 1) on Intimidate checks. In addition, a mindwyrm mesmer never takes penalties on Intimidate checks for being smaller than his target." replace="Consummate liar"}

::ab[Phantasmagorical Breath (Su)]{l=1 icon=cone-line flavor="A mindwyrm mesmer has learned to mimic a ‹family/true dragon«'s› destructive breath using his psychic ability." useMod=Charisma useM=1 standard="A mindwyrm mesmer can unleash his phantasmagoric breath in either a 30-foot cone or a 60-foot line. The breath induces a psychosomatic response in creatures caught in the area, dealing 1d6 points of energy damage. The mindwyrm mesmer selects the type of energy damage this deals (acid, cold, electricity, or fire) when this ability is first gained; once made, this choice can't be changed. Creatures affected can attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the mindwyrm mesmer's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier) to realize the true nature of the attack, which negates all damage. This is a mind-affecting effect." incrementPlain="The energy damage becomes~1~2" incrementEnd="d6." replace="Painful stare"}
::ab[Draconic Trick (Su)]{head icon=power note="The following new trick can be selected by a mindwyrm mesmer."}

::ab[Threatening Mien]{next icon=lower ability="The subject's actions appear to be lethal in the extreme. A mindwyrm mesmer can trigger this trick when the subject moves adjacent to an enemy. Enemies adjacent to the subject take a -4 penalty on attacks against targets other than the subject. This trick lasts for 1 round per mindwyrm mesmer's mesmerist level."}
::ab[Masterful Draconic Trick (Su)]{head icon=power l12="The following new masterful trick can be selected by a mindwyrm mesmer of 12th level or higher."}

::ab[Frightful Countenance]{next icon=def-lower ability="The subject's appearance changes into that which frightens an attacker to the core. The mindwyrm mesmer can trigger this trick when the subject is targeted by an attack or by a spell that requires an attack roll. The attacker must succeed at a Will save or the attack misses and the attacker immediately becomes ‹misc/shaken› for 1d4 rounds. This is a mind-affecting fear effect."}

projectionist.name: Projectionist
description: ## Projectionist
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/18›
Not content to simply command others from afar, a projectionist is able to use his mesmerizing powers to utterly dominate other creatures and objects by projecting his spirit from his body.

::ab[Spells]{icon=down-learn ability="A projectionist's psychic powers manifest in an almost predictable manner, making them less versatile than other spellcasters. At each of the indicated levels, a projectionist must choose the indicated spell to learn - he cannot choose other spells. The spells that a projectionist must learn (and the level at which he must choose them) are shown below." s4="Enter image" s7="Lesser object possession" s10="Riding possession" s13="Object possession" s16="Greater object possession~ (as a 6th-level spell)" alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Implant Consciousness (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A projectionist can spend one use of his mesmerist trick ability to create a psychic link between himself and one object that bears his likeness, such as a painting or a statue. This functions in all ways like implanting a mesmerist trick in a creature, except the target is an object. The projectionist can trigger the trick as a @HLstandard_action at any time - upon doing so, his consciousness leaves his body and fills the target object, functioning as described by the &quot&filling an image with your consciousness&quot& use of the *enter image* spell. Use the projectionist's mesmerist level as the spell's caster level. While filling an image with his consciousness, the projectionist can use and maintain his *hypnotic stare* on opponents, and can also trigger his *painful stare.*" imp8="He can also use any purely mental action (including casting psychic spells) while filling an object with his consciousness." special="Once he implants this trick in an object, the projectionist can fill the target with his consciousness whenever he pleases, regardless of distance or whether he is on the same plane as his target. Once he stops concentrating on maintaining the *enter image* effect, his consciousness returns to his body and he cannot fill the target with his consciousness again until he implants another mesmerist trick in that object." replace="Mesmerist tricks gained at 4th and 8th levels"}

::ab[Hidden Presence]{l=5 icon=power ability="A projectionist gains ‹feat/Hidden Presence› as a bonus feat. He doesn't need to meet the feat's prerequisites. He benefits from Hidden Presence and feats that list Hidden Presence as a prerequisite while possessing objects (as with *object possession*) or while filling an object with his consciousness (as described in the *enter image* spell), as well as when he possesses creatures, provided the feat is applicable. For instance, ‹feat/Intrusive Presence› cannot be used to read the mind of an inanimate object possessed via *enter image* or *object possession,* as objects possess no minds to be read." replace="Mental potency"}

spirit_walker.name: Spirit Walker
description: ## Spirit Walker
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/97›
Some mesmerists specialize in dominating and controlling undead rather than the living.

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn note="A spirit walker adds additional spells to his spell list at the spell levels indicated. He can choose these as spells known, but doesn't gain them automatically." s1="Hide from undead" s2="Command undead" s3="Halt undead" s4="Speak with haunt" alter="Spell list"}

::ab[Undead Inception (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="A spirit walker can affect undead creatures with his *hypnotic stare* as the *psychic inception* ‹ability/bold »stare<s› ability. This allows him to affect only undead; he must actually choose the *psychic inception bold stare* improvement if he wants to also affect other mindless creatures." imp3="When the spirit walker uses this ability, undead targets don't receive a saving throw bonus (if they did to begin with), have only a 25% chance of ignoring the spirit walker's mind-affecting spells and abilities, and no longer need to be under the spirit walker's gaze to be affected." imp5="When the spirit walker uses his mind-affecting spells and abilities, they affect all undead normally, with no chance of failure." replace="Consummate liar~mental potency"}

::ab[Continued Animation (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic immediate="When a spirit walker is using *hypnotic stare* on a humanoid target and the target dies, the spirit walker can force the target's soul to remain in the body. The spirit walker can then control the target as if using ‹spell/dominate person› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 the spirit walker's class level. The creature gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to 2 &times& the spirit walker's class level and continues acting as though it were alive and conscious until it loses those temporary hit points or the duration expires. During this time, the creature counts as undead, but it can't be healed by negative energy." useInc="3~mesmerist" special="The target receives a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the spirit walker's class level + his Charisma modifier) to avoid this effect and die normally. This ability ends immediately if at any point the target's body is completely destroyed (such as by ‹spell/disintegrate›) or restored to life." imp14="The spirit walker can apply this ability to any type of target as if using ‹spell/dominate monster›." replace="3rd- and 14th-level touch treatments"}

::ab[Command Undead (Su)]{l=6 icon=power ability="A spirit walker gains ‹feat/Command Undead› as a bonus feat. A spirit walker can use Command Undead a number of times per day equal to 1 + his Charisma modifier (minimum 1), using his mesmerist level as his cleric level." imp10="The DC to resist this effect increases by 2." replace="6th- and 10th-level touch treatments"}

::ab[Master of the Dead (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="A spirit walker gains mastery of the undead. Once per day, a spirit walker can use ‹spell/control undead› as a spell-like ability on one target. If that creature fails its saving throw and has 20 or fewer Hit Dice, it falls permanently under the spirit walker's control until the spirit walker dies. A creature that fails its save but has more than 20 HD suffers the effect of *command undead.* A creature that succeeds at its save can't be affected by *master of the dead* for 24 hours. A spirit walker can control only one undead creature in this fashion at a time. If he permanently controls a new undead creature, the previous one is set free." replace="Rule minds"}

thought_eater.name: Thought Eater
description: ## Thought Eater
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/38›

Mesmerists that learn to pierce deep into the minds of their victims are known as thought eaters, dangerous individuals capable of devouring thoughts and assuming the knowledge those notions represent for themselves.

::ab[Assume Identity (Su)]{icon=boost ability="When a thought eater targets a creature with his *hypnotic stare* ability, the thought eater gains a bonus on Disguise checks to appear as that creature. This bonus is equal to the thought eater's mesmerist level, and lasts for 24 hours, or until he uses his *hypnotic stare* ability on a different creature." replace="Consummate liar"}

::ab[Assume Morality (Su)]{l=2 icon=magic ability="The thought eater's alignment matches that of the target of his *assume identity* ability for the purposes of spells and effects that discern alignment. This includes any increase to alignment aura strength from class features, such as a cleric's aura ability." replace="Towering ego"}

::ab[Assume Knowledge (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="The thought eater can choose one Knowledge skill possessed by the target of his *hypnotic stare.* He uses the target's number of ranks in that skill, instead of his own, to calculate his bonus (but not for other purposes, such as meeting the prerequisites of feats) to a maximum number of ranks equal to his mesmerist level. This ability lasts for 24 hours or until he uses his *hypnotic stare* ability on a different creature." replace="Touch treatment"}

::ab[Consume Identity (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic free="When the thought eater kills a target of his *hypnotic stare,* he can choose to entirely consume the creature's identity, mind, body, and soul. The target's corpse dissolves and as long as the thought eater lives, the target of *consume identity* cannot be brought back from the dead until the thought eater releases the identity (a @HLstandard_action) or uses *consume identity* on another target. The creature's soul can also be released by a ‹spell/wish› or ‹spell/miracle› (which ends the effect of *consume identity*). When the thought eater has consumed an identity, all divination spells identify him as (and consider him to be) the creature whose identity he consumed rather than himself. This ability fools any spell short of ‹spell/discern location›. *Discern location* pinpoints the thought eater's location, whenever either the thought eater or the consumed creature is the target of that spell." replace="Rule minds"}

toxitician.name: Toxitician
description: ## Toxitician
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/98›
Toxiticians forgo the mesmerist's stare, instead combining their psychic power with their alchemical knowledge to craft injections that torment their foes and bolster themselves and their allies.
::ab[Deft Fingers (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A toxitician adds 1/2 his class level (minimum 1) as a bonus on all Sleight of Hands checks." replace="Consummate liar"}
:::ab{l=1 icon=magic-touch action=Ability title="Injections (Su)" replace="Hypnotic stare" imp8="The penalty on Will saves becomes -3."}

The toxitician uses his skill with alchemy and his own psychic powers to create injections. The toxitician can create a number of injections per day equal to his mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier. It takes 1 hour to create the injections. An injection is inert until the toxitician attempts to use it, taking a @HL[swift action] to infuse it with his psychic power to activate it.

Using an injection requires taking a @HL[standard action] to attempt a melee touch attack. If the attack hits, the target of an injection takes a -2 penalty on its Will saving throws for a number of minutes equal to 1/2 the toxitican's class level (minimum 1). The toxitician can simultaneously attempt a DC 20 Sleight of Hand check to inject the target surreptitiously. If the toxitician uses Sleight of Hand, the target can attempt a Perception check to detect the attempt, opposed by the Sleight of Hand check result, but detecting the attempt doesn't prevent the creature from being injected. Whenever the toxitician uses Sleight of Hand to administer one of his injections, he can implant it rather than injecting it and then trigger the effect of the injection at a later time. At any point before he regains his injections, the toxitician can trigger an implanted injection as a @HL[free action]. The toxitician must be within medium range (100 feet + 10 feet per mesmerist level) of the target of the implanted injection.

If either the melee touch attack or the Sleight of Hand check (if attempted) fails, the injection isn't delivered but can still be used again - once activated, it remains potent for 1 day. An injection that hasn't been administered becomes inert if it leaves the toxitician's possession, reactivating as soon as it returns to his keeping.

The penalties from multiple injections don't stack, nor do the penalties from injections stack with penalties from mesmerist *stares* or witches' ‹hex/evil eye› hexes. Injections are mind-affecting effects.

::ab[Painful Injection (Su)]{icon=boost-lower ability="The toxitician's injection causes intense pain. This increases the damage the subject takes in the same way as the mesmerist's *painful stare* class feature. This extra damage is dealt automatically, without being triggered by the toxitician, but doesn't increase when the toxitician damages the target and can still be dealt only once per round." replace="Painful stare"}

::ab[Injection Improvement (Su)]{head l=3 icon=power order=af ability="A toxitician can select one improvement (from those listed below) to add to his injections. The toxitician doesn't have to select the improvements when he prepares his injections at the start of the day; the choice is made as part of the @HLswift_action to activate the injection. The target can attempt a Fortitude saving throw to resist an injection with an improvement added to it. The DC is 10 + 1/2 the toxitician's class level + the toxitician's Charisma modifier. On a successful save, the target still takes the penalty on its Will saving throws, but not the effects of the improvement." free="A toxitician can sacrifice one use of his injections to increase the DC of another injection by 2." special="The toxitician can't choose the same improvement more than once." incrementOrd="He can select~3~4" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" improvement." replace="Bold stare"}
::ab[Ability Decrease]{next icon=lower ability="The target takes a -2 penalty to one ability score of the toxitician's choosing." imp11="The penalty becomes -4."}
::ab[Excruciating]{next icon=boost ability="The extra damage from *painful injection* increases by an amount equal to 2 points + 1 point per 3 mesmerist levels the toxitician possesses."}
::ab[Fortitude Decrease]{next icon=lower ability="The target's immune system weakens, imposing a -2 penalty on Fortitude saving throws." imp11="The penalty becomes -4."}
::ab[Natural Armor Decrease]{next icon=lower ability="The target's skin becomes looser, decreasing its natural armor (if any) by 2." imp11="Its natural armor bonus instead decreases by 4."}
::ab[Reflex Decrease]{next icon=lower ability="The target's body becomes stiffer, imposing a -2 penalty on the target's Reflex saving throws." imp11="The penalty becomes -4."}

::ab[Slow]{next icon=lower ability="The target's actions become sluggish, reducing its base movement by 10 feet and imposing a -1 penalty on attack rolls." imp11="The penalty on attack rolls becomes -2."}

::ab[Treatment Vials (Su)]{l=3 icon=aid ability="A toxitician can create treatment vials when he creates his injections for the day; creating each treatment vial expends one use of *touch treatment.* A treatment vial has the same benefit as his *touch treatment,* but anyone can administer it as a @HLstandard_action. A vial can remove any condition the toxitician is able to remove at his mesmerist level, but can't be used for a *break enchantment* effect. A treatment vial becomes inert after 24 hours." alter="Touch treatment"}
::ab[Improved Injections (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="The toxitician can add two different improvements to an injection. The injection still requires only one save." replace="Glib lie"}

umbral_mesmerist.name: Umbral Mesmerist
description: ## Umbral Mesmerist
‹SOURCE Occult Realms/16›
Trained in secret, umbral mesmerists are clandestine weapons who spend years learning how to shape terrifying creatures from the inky darkness in which they revel.

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down note="An umbral mesmerist can cast one fewer spell of each level than normal. If this reduces the number of spells for a level to 0, he can cast spells of that level only if his Charisma score allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level." alter=Spellcasting}

:::ab{title="Shadow Summoning (Sp)" action=Ability useMod=Charisma3 icon=magic replace="Mesmerist tricks gained at 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th levels" imp4="This power of this ability increases to ‹spell/summon monster II›." imp8="This power increases to ‹spell/summon monster III›." imp12="This ability increases to that of ‹spell/summon monster IV›." imp16="This power increases to ‹spell/summon monster V›." imp20="This power of this ability increases to ‹spell/summon monster VI›."}

An umbral mesmerist gains the ability to summon quasi-real creatures as per the ‹spell/summon monster I› spell. The umbral mesmerist can use this ability as a @HL[standard action], and the creatures remain for 1 minute per level (instead of 1 round per level). The creatures summoned are only partly real, but have normal effects unless a creature affected by the illusion succeeds at a Will save to disbelieve (as per ‹spell/shadow conjuration›.) The DC for this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 the umbral mesmerist's mesmerist level + the umbral mesmerist's Charisma modifier. This is an illusion with the shadow descriptor.

A shadow creature has half the hit points of a normal creature of its kind (regardless of whether it is recognized as a shadow creature). It deals the normal amount of damage and has all of the base creature's normal abilities and weaknesses. Against a creature that recognizes it as a shadow creature, however, the shadow creature's damage is halved, and all special abilities that do not deal lethal damage are only 50% likely to work. (Roll for each use and each affected character separately.) Furthermore, the shadow creature's AC bonuses are halved. A creature that succeeds at its save to disbelieve sees the summoned creature as transparent images superimposed on vague, shadowy forms. Objects automatically succeed at their Will saves against this spell. The umbral mesmerist cannot use this ability to summon creatures from more than one *summon monster* spell in this way at one time.

::ab[Umbral Solipsism (Su)]{l=2 icon=lower passive="When a creature attempts a Will save to disbelieve a quasi-real creature created by *shadow summoning,* it takes a penalty on the save equal to the mesmerist's Charisma modifier (minimum 0)." replace="Towering ego"}

::ab[Ephemeral Stare (Su)]{l=5 icon=lower ability="The umbral mesmerist is treated as if he were invisible by the target of his *hypnotic stare* (as per the ‹spell/invisibility› spell), provided neither he nor any creatures conjured by *shadow summoning* take hostile actions against the target. Once the umbral mesmerist or any creature conjured by his *shadow summoning* ability takes a hostile action against the target, the mesmerist becomes visible to the target and the target cannot be affected again by that umbral mesmerist's *ephemeral stare* ability for 24 hours (although it can be affected by other effects of his *hypnotic stare*)." replace="Mental potency"}

vexing_daredevil.name: Vexing Daredevil
description: ## Vexing Daredevil
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/98›
Vexing daredevils train their psychic powers for combat, specializing in momentarily blinding and confusing their foes with sudden feints and tricks.
::cskill{gain=acro}
::ab[Martial Weapon Proficiency (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power choice="A vexing daredevil picks one martial weapon and becomes proficient in its use." replace="1st-level mesmerist trick"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l3="A vexing daredevil gains ‹feat/Improved Feint› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet its prerequisites." l6="She gains ‹feat/Greater Feint› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet its prerequisites." l10="She gains ‹feat/Greater Mesmerizing Feint› as a bonus feat, and doesn't need to meet its prerequisites." l14="She gains a bonus ‹main/feats_>stare« feat›; she **must** meet the prerequisites of that feat." replace="Touch treatment"}

::ab[Dazzling Feint (Su)]{head l=3 icon=power ability="Each time a vexing daredevil successfully feints against a creature under the effect of her *hypnotic stare,* she also chooses one of her *dazzling feint* benefits to activate. Unless otherwise stated, a *dazzling feint's* benefit applies only on the vexing daredevil's next attack this round." incrementOrd="She selects~-1~4~1" incrementDesc=her incrementEnd=" *dazzling feint.*" special="The DC for any saving throw against a *dazzling feint's* effect is equal to 10 + 1/2 the vexing daredevil's class level + her Charisma modifier. The vexing daredevil can't choose the same *dazzling feint* more than once." replace="Bold stare"}

::ab[Blinding Strike]{next icon=lower ability="The vexing daredevil's weapon emits a bright flash of light into the opponent's eyes. If the vexing daredevil's next attack hits, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw or be ‹misc/blinded› for 1 round."}

::ab[Combat Maneuver]{next icon=def ability="The vexing daredevil's attack patterns become more difficult to discern. The vexing daredevil chooses one ‹rule/combat maneuver<s› when making this *dazzling feint.* For the rest of the round, she doesn't provoke an attack of opportunity when performing that combat maneuver against the target of her *dazzling feint.* This *dazzling feint* benefit applies on combat maneuvers instead of on the vexing daredevil's next attack."}

::ab[Critical Strike]{next icon=boost ability="Ever alert, the vexing daredevil hones in on the target's weak points. If her next attack is a critical threat against the target of this *dazzling feint,* the vexing daredevil gains a circumstance bonus on the roll to confirm that critical hit equal to ‹misc/one-third› of his mesmerist level."}

::ab[Outmanuever]{next icon=def ability="The vexing daredevil's darting movements foil the target's attempts to box her in. If her next attack hits, the vexing daredevil gains a +4 circumstance bonus on Acrobatics checks to move through the target's space or threatened area for 1 round. She can also move up to half her speed after the attack hits as a @HLfree_action. If she wants to use Acrobatics to move through the target's threatened area or an enemy's space during this movement, she must reduce her speed or the DC of the Acrobatics check increases by 10, as normal."}

::ab[Piercing Strike]{next icon=boost ability="By psychically amplifying the target's pain, the vexing daredevil causes more damage. If she hits with her next attack and deals damage with her *painful stare,* she increases the damage dice from d6s to d8s."}

::ab[Sloppy Defense]{next icon=boost ability="The vexing daredevil's attacks effortlessly penetrate the target's defenses; she gains a circumstance bonus on her next attack roll equal to one-fifth of his mesmerist level."}

::ab[Surprise Strike]{next icon=power prereq="7th-level mesmerist" ability="The vexing daredevil creates an opening so she can squeeze in another strike. If her next attack hits, she can make another attack at her highest attack bonus - 5 as a @HLfree_action."}

::ab[Shimmering Body (Sp)]{l=11 icon=def passive="When a vexing daredevil moves in a round, until the beginning of her next turn, any target of her *hypnotic stare* sees her as a constantly shimmering form of light and treats her as if she were under a ‹spell/blur› spell." replace="Glib lie"}

vexing_trickster.name: Vexing Trickster
description: ## Vexing Trickster
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/19›

While many mesmerists are called to embrace the art of deception, using their mind-binding powers to make their foes believe that even the most outrageous realities are actually true, others indulge in the various hijinks their power enables. These mesmerists are known as vexing tricksters.
::ab[Consummate Trickster (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A vexing trickster gains a +1 bonus on Bluff, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth checks." increment="This bonus~1~4" replace="Consummate liar"}

::ab[Trickster's Ego (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A vexing trickster gains ‹feat/Combat Expertise› as a bonus feat. If his Intelligence score is less than 13, his Intelligence counts as 13 for the purpose of meeting the prerequisites for combat feats." replace="Towering ego"}

::ab[Bonus Feat (Ex)]{icon=power l3="A vexing trickster gains a ‹main/feats_>trick« feat› as a bonus feat. He must meet the feat's prerequisites." l10="He gains a second trick feat as a bonus feat." replace="Touch treatment (minor)~touch treatment (greater)"}

::ab[Manifold Hijinks (Su)]{l=6 icon=power ability="The vexing trickster learns to implant multiple mesmerist tricks that have been modified by trick feats (henceforth called modified tricks) in his targets' minds simultaneously. The vexing trickster can implant up to two modified mesmerist tricks in a single target simultaneously." imp14="He can implant up to three modified mesmerist tricks in a single target simultaneously." special="This ability doesn't allow a mesmerist to apply more than one trick feat to a single mesmerist trick at a time." replace="Touch treatment (moderate)~touch treatment (break enchantment)"}

vizier.name: Vizier
description: ## Vizier
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/38›

While many mesmerists focus on beguiling their foes or taking overt control of others with their mental powers, viziers carve out a subtler niche for themselves as advisors. An adventuring vizier is delighted that enemies focus on the raging barbarian or spell-slinging wizard first, not realizing the threat posed by the vizier until it's too late.

::ab[Insidious Influence (Su)]{flavor="A vizier subtly influences his allies and others who trust him, making it more likely they will follow his suggestions." icon=lower passive="Allies with mesmerist tricks implanted by the vizier take penalties on Will saves made to resist the vizier's abilities and spells, and on Diplomacy, Intimidate, Sense Motive checks attempted against the vizier. This penalty equals that imposed by the vizier's *hypnotic stare.* If the vizier uses his *hypnotic stare* against such an ally, these penalties stack." imp3="The vizier automatically gains the *susceptibility bold stare* improvement, and it also applies to the DCs of any influence check to manipulate the target (even if it isn't Diplomacy or Intimidate)." replace="Consummate liar~bold stare improvement gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Power Behind the Throne (Su)]{l=2 icon=magic-def flavor="The vizier can use his mesmerist powers to make it seem like he is just a hanger-on who isn't contributing to a combat or social situation. This includes making it seem like one of his allies is casting his spells instead." order=an ability="When the vizier casts a spell, once the vizier's spell is complete, a glamer subtly alters onlookers' memories and perception of the spell's casting such that all the usual manifestations of the spell indicate that the caster was one of the vizier's allies with an implanted trick. Creatures who observed the spellcasting can attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the vizier's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier) to disbelieve the glamer." note="Since this effect only comes into play after the spell's casting is complete, any attempts to ‹rule/ready an action› against the spellcasting, ‹rule/counterspell<s›, or take attacks of opportunity still work as normal. This ability does not prevent or otherwise hinder creatures from correctly targeting such attacks and abilities against the vizier (for instance, a creature readying an action to shoot an arrow to interrupt the spellcasting would still shoot the vizier). Additionally, when the vizier triggers an ally's implanted trick, flashy effects make it appear as if the ally was using an activated ability of her own." imp5="The vizier doesn't provoke attacks of opportunity when using *power behind the throne,* as if under the effects of a constant ‹spell/illusion of calm›. A creature that disbelieves this second effect becomes immune to that vizier's *illusion of calm* for 24 hours." replace="Towering ego~mental potency"}

vox.name: Vox
description: ## Vox
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/39›

Most mesmerists practice their mental manipulation through their entrancing gaze, but others have learned to practice their skill through the lilt and intonation of their voice. From sibilant whispers to overwhelming imprecations that resonate with the force of pure will, a vox unleashes the power of his mind with every silken syllable and shattering shout.

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn note="A vox adds the following spells to his class list." s1="Ear-piercing scream" s2="Cacophonous call~~disrupt silence~~distracting cacophony~~piercing shriek~~sonic scream" s4="Shout~~sonic thrust~~wall of sound" s5="Mass cacophonous call~~echolocation~~resonating word" s6="Magnifying chime~~greater shout~~sonic form" special="Unlike most psychic spells, a vox's mesmerist spells use both thought and verbal components; like a ‹class/bard›, a vox cannot remove the verbal components of his spells." alter=Spellcasting replace="Towering ego~10th-level mesmerist trick~touch treatment (break enchantment)"}

::ab[Compelling Voice (Su)]{icon=lower swift="A vox can focus the sound of his voice on one creature within 30 feet, sapping that target's willpower and rendering her more vulnerable to mind-affecting powers, rather than using a stare like other mesmerists." special="A vox can use his *compelling voice* even while ‹misc/deafened›, but it has a 20% chance to fail, just like casting a spell with a verbal component. The target does not need to be able to understand what the vox is saying, but does need to be able to hear the sound of the vox's voice (for instance, this ability cannot be used if either the vox or his target is in an area of magical silence). Otherwise, the vox's *compelling voice* has the effects of the standard mesmerist's *hypnotic stare,* and it gains further benefits as normal from abilities like the *painful stare* and *bold stare* class features." alter="Hypnotic stare"}

::ab[Wounding Words (Su)]{l=3 icon=touch-melee-lower flavor="A vox can focus the power of his voice to magnify the force of his blows in melee." standard="A vox can either make a melee touch attack that deals 1d6 points of sonic damage plus 1 point per mesmerist level, or he can make a single attack with a melee weapon, adding the listed sonic damage to the damage dealt by his weapon on a successful attack." useMod=Charisma3 special="If the target of the vox's *wounding words* is also the target of his *compelling voice,* that creature must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the vox's mesmerist level + his Charisma modifier) or take a -2 penalty on attack rolls, saving throws, skill checks, and ability checks for 1 round. This penalty doesn't stack with the effects of the ‹feat/Concussive Spell› metamagic feat." replace="Touch treatment (minor)~touch treatment (moderate)"}

::ab[Subsonic Strike (Su)]{l=10 icon=zap standard="A vox is able to unleash a deadly flurry of his *wounding words* at once. He can target a number of creatures or objects equal to his Charisma modifier (minimum 1) within 30 feet, requiring a ranged touch attack against each target. If the target of his *compelling voice* is within 30 feet, he can choose to instead target only that creature with a single ranged touch or melee touch attack, dealing double normal damage on a successful attack." special="Whatever choice he makes, the vox must expend one use of *wounding words.*" replace="Touch treatment (major)"}
